I've reviewed all three fixes against the task spec by reading the actual source (the extension is untracked, so `git diff` only surfaces `install.sh`; the fix files I read directly).

## Verification of the three fixes

**FIX 1 ‚Äî mark only mined transcripts (learn.ts):** `buildUpdaterTask` now returns `{ task, included }` (learn.ts:108-141). First transcript is always included (`included.length > 0 && ‚Ä¶` guard at learn.ts:122 is false on the first iteration), so a single oversized transcript still progresses. `runLearningPass` refreshes the index from `included` only (learn.ts:202-204) and `deltaCount = included.length`. Over-cap deltas re-appear via `selectDeltaTranscripts`. Correct.

**FIX 2 ‚Äî failure must not advance/ must surface (learn.ts + index.ts):** Index refresh moved after the updater and gated on `result.exitCode === 0` (learn.ts:201-205); old pre-write removed (confirmed: no `deltas.map` index write, no "survive a crash" comment remain). Throw path returns `ok:false` without touching the index (learn.ts:197-200). `LearnRunOutcome` gains `ok` + optional `stderr` (learn.ts:145-152); failure `reason` carries the stderr tail (learn.ts:163-166). `ensureLearnedScaffold` still runs before the updater (learn.ts:191). index.ts surfaces failures with a warning on both the cadence path (index.ts:92-95) and the command (index.ts:116-123), and the success guard now also requires `outcome.ok` (index.ts:94). Correct.

**FIX 3 ‚Äî cadence fires in normal use (cadence.ts + README):** `DEFAULT_MIN_MINUTES = 30`, `DEFAULT_MIN_TURNS = 10` (cadence.ts:51-52); AND semantics and trial logic untouched. cadence_test default assertion updated to 30; minutes-gate test still uses explicit `opts({minMinutes:120})`. README says "10 turns and 30 minutes". Correct.

**Tests:** learn_test.sh covers the non-zero-exit no-advance case, the retry-after-failure, the 8-then-1 batch drain, and the oversized-first-transcript inclusion. All consistent with the code.

No external consumers import the changed symbols; no stale `120`/pre-write references remain. The fixes are minimal, faithful, and within scope.

## install.sh (only item in `git diff`)
This change is **not part of this task** (the bug-fix handoff and log mention only the three fixes; the whole extension is untracked scaffolding). It switches `~/.pi/agent/agents` from a whole-dir symlink to a real dir of per-file symlinks. It has a latent upgrade-path bug, but it is out of scope for this review and not this task's work, so I'm not blocking on it ‚Äî flagging for awareness only.

REVIEW: APPROVE

NITS:
- install.sh:44-51 (out of scope / pre-existing, not this task): on upgrade from the old layout `~/.pi/agent/agents` is a symlink to `extensions/foreman/crew`; `mkdir -p` follows it instead of replacing it, so the subsequent per-file `link` calls resolve through the symlink and `mv` real repo files to `.bak-‚Ä¶` inside `extensions/foreman/crew`, mutating the repo. Worth replacing the symlink (e.g. `rm` if `[ -L ]`) before `mkdir -p` ‚Äî but unrelated to the three reliability fixes.
